methods.description: The presence of cellular markers of proliferation (PCNA) and apoptosis (BAX) was detected by immunocytochemistry. This method involves using specific monoclonal primary antibodies against these molecules and secondary swine antibodies against mouse IgG labelled with horseradish peroxidase (HRP) for detection via diaminobenzidine (DAB) substrate. Verification was performed using secondary polyclonal goat antibodies against mouse IgG labelled with fluorescein isothiocyanate (FITC). Staining was assessed using a fluorescent microscope, and the proportion of positive cells was calculated.

steps.description: Specifically, monoclonal primary antibodies against these molecules (Santa Cruz Biotechnology, Inc., Santa Cruz, CA, USA; diluted 1:500 in PBS) were used.
and secondary swine antibodies against mouse IgG labelled with horseradish peroxidase (Servac, Prague, Czech Republic; diluted 1:1000) were used.
inputs: Secondary swine antibodies against mouse IgG (HRP-labelled)

steps.searchable_summary: Incubate cells with diluted primary antibodies
Incubate cells with diluted HRP-labelled secondary antibodies
outputs: Cells with HRP-labelled secondary antibodies bound

steps.description: cells were stained with diaminobenzidine (DAB) substrate (Roche Diagnostics GmbH, Manheim, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: Stain cells using DAB substrate for HRP detection

steps.description: In addition, the presence of the primary antibodies was verified in some cells by secondary polyclonal goat antibodies against mouse IgG labelled with fluorescein isothiocyanate (FITC) (Santa Cruz Biotechnology, Inc.; diluted 1:10,000).
steps.searchable_summary: Dilute FITC-labelled secondary antibodies for verification
inputs: Secondary polyclonal goat antibodies against mouse IgG (FITC-labelled)

steps.description: In addition, the presence of the primary antibodies was verified in some cells by secondary polyclonal goat antibodies against mouse IgG labelled with fluorescein isothiocyanate (FITC) (Santa Cruz Biotechnology, Inc.; diluted 1:10,000).
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate cells with FITC-labelled secondary antibodies for verification
inputs: Cells with primary antibodies bound (or separate samples)
outputs: Cells with FITC-labelled secondary antibodies bound (fluorescent signal)

steps.description: Cells processed without primary or secondary antibodies were used as negative controls.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare negative control samples for immunocytochemistry

steps.description: Staining in the cells was assessed using a fluorescent microscope (M205 FCA, Leica Microsystems GmbH, Wetzlar, Germany) equipped with specific wavelength filters for fluorescein-5isothiocyanate (FITC) and 4',6-diamidino-2-phenylindole (DAPI) and a PlanApo dry objective at 20x magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: Image stained cells using fluorescent microscopy

steps.description: Images were acquired using a DFC 480 camera...
...and processed using the Leica IM500 software.
Cells expressing a signal greater than what was observed in the background negative control experiments were considered to have a positive signal.
steps.searchable_summary: Define positive signal based on negative control background
inputs: Processed images of negative control cells

steps.description: The proportion of cells containing visible molecules relative to the total number of cells was then calculated.
